highlights.description: New `asset` package for uploading audio, images, and video to HeyGen's asset service (upload.heygen.com)

added.description: `asset.Client` with `Upload` returning a hosted URL usable in other HeyGen APIs (e.g., audio-driven video generation)
`asset.ContentType*` constants for the supported asset types (JPEG, PNG, MP4, WebM, MPEG audio)
`Client.Asset` facade field on the root SDK client
`heygen.Client.RequestURL` for raw-body requests against absolute URLs (endpoints hosted outside the API base URL)
`avatar.ListV2` and `avatar.SearchV2` for the v2 avatars API, returning IDs directly usable as avatar_id in generation (unlike the v3 avatar-group IDs)

`omniavatar` subpackage: an OmniAvatar render adapter implementing the omniavatar-core render interfaces (`render.Provider`, `render.AudioUploader`, `render.AvatarLister`) on top of this SDK; depends only on omniavatar-core
Unit tests for the asset upload client
Unit tests for `RequestURL` and retry body rewind

fixed.description: Retries now rewind the request body via `GetBody`, so a 429-retried POST resends the full payload instead of an empty body

documentation.description: README: asset upload feature, usage example, and API coverage row
MkDocs site: Asset API page, nav entry, and index/overview updates
Adopt the shared PlexusOne MkDocs theme (custom palette, unified navigation override)

highlights.description: Initial release of the HeyGen Go SDK with Avatar and LiveAvatar API support

added.description: Core HTTP client with retry logic and exponential backoff
Main SDK entry point with `heygen.New()` constructor
LITE mode support for BYO AI stack with LiveKit integration
Sandbox mode with `SandboxAvatarID` for testing without credits
Environment variable support for API keys (`HEYGEN_API_KEY`, `LIVEAVATAR_API_KEY`)

documentation.description: README with quick start guide and usage examples
MkDocs documentation site with guides and API reference
LiveAvatar session example demonstrating full flow

tests.description: Unit tests for avatar, heygen, and liveavatar packages
